# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application. Use the official Node.js base image and install dependencies with npm install. The app listens on port 3000. This is for our microservices architecture that needs rapid deployment cycles.
# Category: container_security

# Start from node alpine to take advantage of a small size, then define the base image as it runs in memory and provides faster startup times with larger images but less disk space compared to complete repos or no root filesystem provided by default that is missing. – https://hub.docker.com/_/node
FROM node:alpine AS builder
# Copy local code into container at /app directory, this will be our actual work volume; build a package with npm install and save it in the cache layer (builder). - Reference to an existing project or its context using git source URLs. – https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#add-a-file
COPY . /app
WORKDIR /app
RUN ["npm", "install"] # Install Node dependencies in the runtime environment, run npm install at base image so that they can be reused later by other containers too - https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#understand-build-args-from-the-d-separate-file
EXPOSE 3000 # Expose port (make sure you use the same port for this container, otherwise it won’t work). In production environments most likely not need to expose ports unless there's a specific requirement. - https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#expose
CMD ["node", "server"] # This is just defining what command gets executed when running the image (here it’ll be node server). It won’t start if no CMD statement was present in Dockerfile - https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#cmd-run
